codeReview.summary: Excellent implementation of concurrent worker support for choir-in-choir. The feature correctly enables parents to spawn multiple children that run concurrently with independent channels and isolated results. The implementation adds auto-notification mechanisms (updateWorkItemState and notifyParent) properly integrated into all task execution paths (executeWithProvider, executeWithToolLoop, handleExecutionError). Comprehensive test coverage with 16 test cases validates all VAL-CHOIR-008 requirements including concurrent spawning, simultaneous execution, independent completion, channel isolation, no sibling interference, work item sync, and health reporting.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The concurrent-workers.md library documentation was created with excellent detail covering: auto-notification behavior, work item state sync, channel-based parent notification, concurrency model (goroutines, WAL mode, MaxOpenConns), and test coverage summary. This is a good pattern for documenting complex runtime features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/concurrent-workers.md - 69 lines covering all key implementation details and gotchas

sharedStateObservations.observation: The test file establishes a strong pattern for concurrent testing: testConcurrentSetup helper creates fresh Runtime with slow StubProvider (500ms delay) to ensure tasks stay running long enough for concurrent observation. This pattern should be referenced for future concurrent feature tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: internal/runtime/concurrent_workers_test.go:33-62 - testConcurrentSetup helper with slow provider configuration

sharedStateObservations.observation: The conditionalFailProvider test helper demonstrates a useful pattern for testing mixed pass/fail scenarios by matching on prompt content. This enables deterministic failure injection without complex mocking.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: internal/runtime/concurrent_workers_test.go:1178-1205 - conditionalFailProvider that fails tasks containing specific prefix in prompt

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed go-worker skill procedure: wrote 16 comprehensive tests first (TDD), then implemented the feature, updated existing tests to account for auto-notification behavior, and added library documentation. All validators passed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true, handoff.verification.commandsRun all show exitCode: 0

sharedStateObservations.observation: The timing test (TestConcurrentWorkers_TasksActuallyRunConcurrently) uses a smart validation approach: 3 tasks of 200ms each should complete in <500ms if truly concurrent vs ~600ms if serial. This is a reliable pattern for verifying actual concurrency vs serialized execution.

summary: The scheduler-concurrent-workers feature is fully implemented and well-tested. The code correctly handles: (1) Concurrent goroutine spawning - each child runs independently via `go rt.executeTask()`; (2) Goroutine management - tracked in `rt.running` map with cancel functions, proper WaitGroup usage in tests; (3) Isolation between siblings - each worker has independent channel, state stored separately, no shared data; (4) Independent result collection - results auto-posted to parent channel on completion via notifyParent(); (5) Resource management - SQLite WAL mode with SetMaxOpenConns(1), stress tests up to 10 concurrent workers pass; (6) Health reporting - RunningCount() correctly reflects concurrent tasks. All 16 new tests pass including race condition detection (-race flag), timing verification, and mixed pass/fail scenarios. Library documentation added to .factory/library/concurrent-workers.md.
